body.$value: A teacher signs in through your third-party SSO / Password Sync setup. They forgot their password and you reset it under Directory > Users > Reset password. Why does the reset fail to help them sign in?
Their actual password lives in the external IdP/AD, not in Google, so the Google reset does not apply
The reset always works; the teacher just needs to clear their browser cache
Admin password resets only fail when the user has 2-Step Verification turned on
The reset failed because the new password did not meet length requirements
A student account was deleted in error. For how long can the account be restored before its data is purged?
5 days
20 days
60 days
Indefinitely, as long as a license is available
You try to restore a recently deleted user but the restore fails. What is the most likely K-12 cause?
More than 20 days have passed, so this is impossible regardless of action
No license is available for the user's previous edition/service, so the restore cannot complete
The user was on third-party SSO, which blocks all restores
Only a super admin in the primary domain can restore users
A staff member is locked out by 2-Step Verification (lost phone). Since only the user can turn 2SV on or off, what is the admin's primary remedy to get them signed in?
Turn off 2-Step Verification for the user from the admin console
Provide a backup verification code (Directory > Users > user > Security > Get backup verification codes)
Reset the user's password, which clears the 2SV requirement
Delete and recreate the account to remove 2SV
A user lost the security key/passkey they used as their second factor. What is the correct admin-and-user sequence to restore access?
The admin enrolls a replacement security key on the user's behalf and hands it to them
The admin transfers the lost key's credential to a new key from the console
The admin removes the lost factor, then the user re-enrolls a new security key/passkey themselves
The user simply buys an identical key model and it works without any re-enrollment
Why must K-12 student password resets route through an administrator rather than student self-recovery?
The 'Allow users and non-super admins to recover their account' setting is unavailable for Education users under age 18
Students are never allowed to have recovery phone numbers on file
Self-recovery works for students but takes up to 20 days to complete
Self-recovery is disabled for everyone on Education editions, students and staff alike
